title: Question: MULTIDRIVEN warning on structs
body:
---


Author Name: **Yosi Arbeli**
Original Redmine Message: 1037 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi:

Thanks for the brilliant tool.

In my Verilog code, I used:
```
typedef struct packed {
  reg [31:0] DataPath;
  reg [7:0]  CtrlPath;
  } my_struct;

my_struct [7:0] array_of_struct;

```

Now, DataPath is a register that I do not want to connect to an async reset signal, whereas CtrlPath is a register that I do want to reset at power up.

To do that I'm using two separate "always@(posedge...)" blocks.

One with reset event for array_of_struct[n].CtrlPath

And another, without hard reset for the array_of_struct[n].DataPath

(In every always block I drive different physical register so that it is not a real "multi drive").

Is there any thing I can do except for putting ``` /* verilator lint_off/on MULTIDRIVEN */ 
```???

Please advise.

Regards,
Yosi A.
